behaviors.description: Renamed files are reported only in `renamed`, not double-counted as `added` + `deleted`.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: environment/external-system plus parser wiring error at the git-output boundary (rename metadata misread as add/delete). Required setup: a real git repo with an actual rename and CLI execution against that repo. Mocked git output can hide real rename encoding details (e.g., rename scores/status formats), so Integration is insufficient for this behavior. Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior text is purely deterministic git/change accounting with no AI/model step.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp repo, commit `a.txt`, rename it to `b.txt` with `git mv`, run the CLI, and assert report shows `renamed=1` and does not increment `added`/`deleted` for that same change.

behaviors.description: Binary diffs do not crash processing and binary files are still counted.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: environment/external-system error (real binary diff output/encoding behavior) that can trigger decode/parsing crashes. Required setup: real repo containing binary file changes and real CLI execution. In-process mocks often use sanitized text and miss NUL-byte/binary diff behavior, so they can pass while production fails. Agentic/Workflow rejected: no AI-dependent correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Create a repo, commit a binary file (e.g., image/random bytes), modify it, run the CLI, and assert process exits successfully and includes the binary change in counts.

behaviors.description: A repository with no commits yields an empty report instead of an error.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: environment/external-system error at repo state boundary (unborn HEAD / initial repo state handling). Required setup: real `git init` repo with zero commits and actual CLI run. Mocking repository state can hide real git error codes/messages from commands that assume `HEAD` exists, so lower levels miss true failures. Agentic/Workflow rejected by behavior text: deterministic CLI behavior only.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize an empty repo with `git init` and no commits, run the CLI, assert zero/empty structured report on stdout and successful non-error behavior.

behaviors.description: Reported counts match the repository’s actual change history.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: module handoff plus external-system error across git history traversal and category aggregation. Required setup: real commit history containing adds/deletes/renames/modifications and end-to-end CLI execution. A mocked history provider can encode expected answers and mask real-history traversal bugs, so System is the minimum to catch real mismatch failures. Agentic/Workflow rejected: no model output quality dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temp repo with a known sequence of commits covering add/delete/rename/modify, run the CLI for that history window, and assert category totals exactly match the ground-truth operations performed.

behaviors.description: Output is identical for equivalent staged vs unstaged changes.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode: environment/external-system error at git index vs working-tree boundary (state interpretation differs by staging). Required setup: real repo with equivalent change sets exercised once unstaged and once staged, then real CLI runs for both. Mocked states can hide subtle git index semantics, so Integration is too low for this specific behavior. Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior is deterministic and non-LLM.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, produce a known change set; run CLI with changes unstaged and capture output; stage the same changes and rerun; assert structured outputs are identical.
